chunks.text:
Kafka is designed for high-throughput append-only log processing and has a mature ecosystem.
Pulsar separates compute and storage, supports multi-tenancy, and is suitable for cloud-native messaging scenarios.
Kafka is often preferred when ecosystem maturity and operational familiarity are priorities.
Pulsar may be preferred when geo-replication and storage-compute separation are important.

Hybrid RAG combines sparse retrieval and dense retrieval.
Sparse retrieval such as BM25 is good for exact keyword matching.
Dense retrieval is better for semantic similarity.
A cross-encoder reranker can improve final relevance by scoring query-document pairs more precisely.

Redis can be used as short-term memory for agent systems.
A common design is to store recent conversation turns, tool traces, and task state in Redis.
Using a sliding window helps control token cost while preserving recent context.
